creator: dave@daveg.ca
text:
(In reply to John W. O'Brien from comment #3)

When I first submitted this bug, epair was not a thing.  I also note that on the machine that started all this, the bridge has mac address 58:9c:fc:10:dc:06, while the interface connected to the switch has mac address f4:52:14:60:a3:52 (and the vlan has the same address --- which I think is correct).

Now, I believe, normally, a switch would have it's own MAC and each port would have another mac.  VLANs don't get macs?

Where I'm going with this thought is that I have multiple bridges on the host --- they each have different macs.  They share vlan connections out to the external switch.  This is _different_ than, say, a cisco that would have the same MAC (I believe) as the "switch" MAC for each vlan.
